item.title: U.S.-Iran peace deal could be finalized within 24 hours, Pakistan says
item.description: President Trump, however, blasted the Iranians for launching attack drones at shipping vessels in the Strait of Hormuz overnight.

item.title: Workers begin removing Trump's name from Kennedy Center
item.description: A federal appellate court denied a last-minute attempt by the Trump administration to stop the removal of President Trump's name from the Kennedy Center on Friday.

item.title: See the full U.S. men's soccer schedule for the 2026 World Cup
item.description: The U.S. men's national soccer team kicked off its 2026 FIFA World Cup campaign against Paraguay at SoFi Stadium on Friday.

item.title: Analyzing a potential U.S., Iran agreement and the economic impact of the war
item.description: The U.S. and Iran appear closer than ever to an agreement that would launch negotiations on a long-term peace deal. CBS News national security contributor Sam Vinograd has analysis, and Linda Bilmes, a senior lecturer at the Harvard Kennedy School, discusses the Iran war's economic impact.

item.title: Trump says U.S. killed Tren de Aragua leader in airstrike in Venezuela
item.description: The U.S. military has killed Niño Guerrero, the alleged leader of Venezuela-based gang Tren de Aragua, President Trump announced Friday.

item.title: U.S. cruises past Paraguay 4-1 in World Cup opener
item.description: The U.S. men's national soccer team made easy work of Paraguay in its World Cup opener Friday, defeating the South American side 4-1 at SoFi Stadium.

item.title: The 2026 FIFA World Cup schedule and how to watch
item.description: With 104 World Cup games being played in the U.S., Canada and Mexico, it's like "a Super Bowl every single day for five weeks," U.S. team captain Tim Ream told CBS News.

item.title: Judge continues to block DOJ's $1.8 billion "anti-weaponization" fund
item.description: A federal judge continued to block the Justice Department's $1.8 billion "anti-weaponization" fund, expressing skepticism with the administration's claims that the program is not moving forward.

item.title: 1 killed, 22 injured in Virginia church tent collapse
item.description: The incident occurred Friday night during an outdoor service at East Lake Community Church in Moneta, Virginia.

item.title: "Face the Nation with Margaret Brennan" guests for June 14, 2026
item.description: This week's guests include Secretary of Defense Pete Hegseth, Democratic Sen. Mark Kelly and former National Economic Council Director Gary Cohn.

item.title: "It rips my heart apart": U.S. military veteran calls on ICE to release wife
item.description: An American citizen who served in the Army and the Texas National Guard for roughly 20 years is desperately urging immigration officials to release his wife, who is facing deportation to Honduras.

item.title: Veteran calls on ICE to release his wife: "My heart broke"
item.description: Retired Staff Sgt. Wilmer Trujillo served in the U.S. National Guard for roughly 20 years. Now, he is calling on federal immigration officials to release his wife, Arelys Barahona-Martinez, a native Honduran who is facing deportation. Trujillo spoke with CBS News' Camilo Montoya-Galvez.

item.title: U.S. monitoring Chinese activity in South China Sea
item.description: Tensions between China and the Philippines, a key American ally, intensified this week. U.S. intelligence agencies are closely monitoring activity around the disputed Scarborough Shoal, according to U.S. officials. The officials say the Chinese government installed what Beijing has called a "scientific structure" in the middle of the reef, angering the Philippines. CBS News' James LaPorta, who broke that story alongside Olivia Gazis, has more.

item.title: Alabama seeks lethal injection execution after court rejects nitrogen gas method
item.description: Alabama is seeking to execute a man with lethal injection hours after his nitrogen execution was prevented from going forward.

item.title: Girls who call themselves twins say, "We have a bond that we think no one else can break"
item.description: Steve Hartman goes "On the Road" again with a pair of twins, who aren't really twins. Just don't try to tell them that.

item.title: Severe storms, tornadoes slam Midwest, killing at least 1
item.description: Severe storms that swept through the Midwest late Thursday knocked out power to hundreds of thousands of customers, damaged buildings and canceled flights.

item.title: At least 1 killed, 9 hospitalized in Texas mass shooting; suspect dead
item.description: The suspect, Victor Mata Villarreal, was wanted for attempted murder of a police officer after shooting at law enforcement during a vehicle chase earlier this week, officials said.

item.title: DOJ paves way for Paramount Skydance to buy Warner Bros. Discovery
item.description: Paramount Skydance's $110 billion acquisition of Warner Bros. Discovery would not harm U.S. consumers or weaken competition, DOJ antitrust enforcers said.

item.title: U.S. to take on Paraguay as World Cup returns to American shores
item.description: The U.S. calls it soccer, much of the world calls it football, but everybody calls it the World Cup. It's bigger than ever, and on Friday night, the competition returns to American shores as the U.S. takes on Paraguay. Nicole Valdes has more from Inglewood, California.

item.title: 6/12: CBS Evening News
item.description: A shooting in Texas kills at least one and injures 10; tornadoes tear through the Midwest, leaving widespread damage.

item.title: Is the U.S. on track to an agreement with Iran?
item.description: A U.S. official said they are 80-85% confident that a deal with Iran will be signed in the coming days, but it's not 100% certain. Washington and Tehran also shared different terms on a potential deal. CBS News political reporter Zak Hudak has more.

item.title: Human remains found in 2 Twin Cities lakes 33 years ago identified
item.description: Investigators in Washington County, Minnesota, say they've identified the human remains found in two Twin Cities lakes more than three decades ago.

item.title: Candidate confusion in Alaska Senate race as Republicans with same name compete for same seat
item.description: Two Republicans with the same name, incumbent Sen. Dan S. Sullivan and challenger Dan J. Sullivan, are battling on the ballot in Alaska. CBS News election law expert and contributor David Becker joins to unpack the campaign confusion.

item.title: Details on SpaceX debut and DOJ approving Paramount merger with Warner
item.description: SpaceX officially went public on Friday, making history as the largest initial public offering of all time and making Elon Musk a trillionaire. Also on Friday, the Justice Department gave the green light for a merger between CBS News' parent company, Paramount Skydance, and Warner Bros. Discovery. Jo Ling Kent has more.

item.title: Justice Department approves Paramount-Warner Bros. merger
item.description: The Justice Department has given the merger between CBS News' parent company, Paramount Skydance, and Warner Bros. Discovery the green light. CBS News MoneyWatch correspondent Kelly O'Grady has the latest.

item.title: U.S. deports Afghani and Iranian migrants to Central African Republic
item.description: A U.S. official tells CBS News that the Trump administration deported about 20 migrants from countries including Afghanistan and Iran on Friday, sending them to the Central African Republic. CBS News immigration correspondent Camilo Montoya-Galvez reports.

item.title: FISA surveillance powers set to expire
item.description: The U.S.' foreign surveillance powers are set to expire Friday after the House rejected a short-term FISA extension, as some lawmakers opposed President Trump's interim pick for director of national intelligence, Bill Pulte. CBS News intelligence and national security reporter Olivia Gazis has the details.

item.title: 6/12: The Takeout with Major Garrett
item.description: Iran's top diplomat says deal "has never been closer"; FISA surveillance set to expire despite new intelligence chief pick.

item.title: U.S. military making plans to secure Iran's nuclear materials, sources say
item.description: Military planners have discussed contingencies that would involve U.S. forces helping secure Iran's nuclear materials if a deal is reached, according to U.S. officials familiar with knowledge of the ongoing planning.

item.title: FDA authorizes emergency use of drug to fight screwworms in pets
item.description: Nitenpyram is the first generic animal drug authorized to treat screwworms in dogs and cats, according to federal regulators.
